housing.summary: Limited direct comments about UK housing exist in these posts, but the university is mentioned as a practical safety school for Kentucky residents. One out-of-state student noted the campus is beautiful but struggled socially. Parking is a specific frustration mentioned by at least one student who considered transferring over it.
key_quotes: I decided to go to the University of Kentucky, and here is my reason for wanting to leave: Parking... I have bought a $300 parking pass, and still paid over 150 in parking violation fees
great place, beautiful campus, but I do not like the atmosphere created by the students and faculty
The only friend I came here with has pretty much ditched me for his fraternity

social_life.summary: Greek life dominates the social scene at UK, and multiple students warn that it's nearly required for men who want to attend parties. The school skews conservative and students who don't fit that mold report feeling out of place. The social environment is a recurring reason students consider transferring.
key_quotes: Down here, being in Greek life is pretty much the only way to get into parties as a guy
I knew that the majority of the student body doesn't conform to my personality--it's a more conservative school with a VERY large greek population
I don't fit in here whatsoever. The only friend I came here with has pretty much ditched me for his fraternity

dining.summary: There is essentially no discussion of dining options at UK across these 316 posts. No students mentioned campus food quality, dining hall options, or meal plan value. This is a notable gap in available student feedback.
key_quotes: No relevant quotes found in the dataset regarding UK dining
No relevant quotes found in the dataset regarding UK dining
No relevant quotes found in the dataset regarding UK dining

mental_health.summary: A student described struggling with unacknowledged depression that derailed their academics at UK, causing their GPA to drop significantly. Another freshman described feeling like they don't belong and being miserable. Mental health challenges appear to be a real but underserved issue on campus based on student accounts.
key_quotes: my last semester at The University of Kentucky was driven off course by some long-run, unacknowledged depression that took over. I quit going to class... my GPA dropped from a 3.4 to a 2.3
I am incredibly unhappy here. I'm just miserable and my emotional state has taken a very sharp decline
I chose the school as a safety backup, and because I didn't really have any better options. So I have just felt like I don't belong here

financial_aid.summary: UK offers competitive merit scholarships that are frequently cited as a reason to attend, including full out-of-state tuition waivers for strong stats. The Singletary scholarship requires a 33 ACT or 4.0 UW GPA for test-optional consideration. For in-state students, UK is generally considered affordable, and one student noted only $6k remaining cost after aid.
key_quotes: 3.5 GPA / 31 ACT or 1420 SAT: Full out of state tuition
Just got my ACT score back and I only got a 32. I was hoping to apply for the University of Kentucky Otis A. Singletary scholarship but you need a 33
His remaining cost for UK was only $6k. Completely doable, just not free like UofL

academics.summary: UK is consistently described as a solid state school safety, not a rigorous academic environment, and students who came from competitive high schools report being underchallenged initially before getting a rude awakening. The STEM program exists but students question its quality compared to peers like Purdue. One student described coming from a small high school expecting an easy ride and being surprised by the difficulty.
key_quotes: I come from a small high school where I barely had to try to get good grades... I get to UK expecting an easy ride
I'm in a STEM program at the University of Kentucky (although it seems like the program is struggling)
Considering as I'll likely have a full ride and they have a decent engineering program for a state school. And it's only undergrad so it's not too much of a big deal

overall_vibe.summary: UK is perceived broadly as a large, party-focused SEC school that is best suited for Kentucky residents who value affordability and familiarity. It's frequently listed as a safety school, and students who arrive expecting a transformative academic experience often feel disappointed. The school has a strong sports culture and is genuinely beloved by students who fit its mold.
key_quotes: My realistic first choice is university of Kentucky, my dream first choice is Yale
ya boi just got into kentucky but just bombed a calc final whos with me going to lexington
University of Kentucky: my in-state school. Much cheaper and affordable... Also has great student life, extracurriculars, party environment I like

red_flags.summary: UK's administration has drawn mockery for accidentally sending acceptance emails to thousands of students who never applied, and the parking office is specifically called out for dishonesty and refusing to correct its own mistakes. Greek life gatekeeping of social events and a conservative campus culture are consistent concerns for students who don't fit that profile.
key_quotes: they lied about having my car impounded. It really shows the colors of the school when they make mistakes but refuse to actually do anything about said mistakes
UKentucky spam emails keep calling me Kathryn (my name is not Kathryn or any other spelling). I do not trust them
being in Greek life is pretty much the only way to get into parties as a guy... I don't fit in here whatsoever

hidden_gems.summary: UK's Honors College is specifically cited as a strong option and a legitimate backup plan even for high-achieving students rejected from top schools. The merit scholarship system is genuinely generous and can make UK nearly free for strong Kentucky applicants. UK also ranks surprisingly high in NCAA basketball prestige, which matters culturally on campus.
key_quotes: UK Honors College (very pleased, great backup plan)
Not T20 or even T100... With that said, they have some very competitive scholarships: 3.5 GPA / 31 ACT or 1420 SAT: Full out of state tuition
Kentucky ranked #3 in Top Universities Ranked by NCAA Division 1 Men's Basketball Tournament Results
